etymology.description: Describes the character as a variant of 魎
etymology.interpretation: This character serves as a variant of 魎(魎) (liǎng, 'spirit') but in modern Chinese, it is predominantly used for the meaning 'two'.

examples.chinese: 我有两本书(我有兩本書)。
他们有两个孩子(他們有兩個孩子)。
我需要两支笔(我需要兩支筆)，用来写字(用來寫字)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xūyào liǎng zhī bǐ, yòng lái xiězì.
examples.english: I need two pens to write.
